_meta.title: HireRise Onboarding API — Phase 1 Contract Spec
_meta.status: FROZEN — additive changes only after frontend sign-off
_meta.auth: All endpoints require Firebase ID token in Authorization: Bearer <token> header, except where noted

shape.errorCode: string — machine-readable code from ErrorCodes registry
shape.userMessage: string | undefined — plain-language copy safe to render in UI. Present when the error has UI-relevant context. When absent, frontend should render a generic fallback message.

POST /consent.description: Step 0 — Record user consent to Terms of Service and Privacy Policy. Idempotent: calling again with the same consentVersion returns the existing record.

consentVersion.description: Version of T&C/Privacy Policy the user accepted. Must exist in consentVersions collection (P0-05).

referralSource.description: Acquisition channel. Invalid values coerced to 'other'.

data.alreadyRecorded: boolean — true when this version was already stored (idempotent replay)

errors.userMessage: This version of our Terms & Privacy Policy has been updated. Please refresh the page and accept the latest version.
errors.when: consentVersion is deprecated in consentVersions collection

POST /quick-start.description: P1-01 — Phase 1 minimum viable data save. Accepts 4 required fields and fires provisional CHI immediately. Replaces the old 7-field wall with a progressive two-phase model. CHI generation is async — response returns immediately with chiStatus: 'generating'.

expectedRoleIds.description: At least one required, UNLESS targetRoleFreeText is provided. Each ID validated against roles collection.

targetRoleFreeText.description: P1-10: Free-text fallback when user cannot find their role in the picker. Stored as-is without Firestore lookup. When provided, expectedRoleIds may be empty.

skills.description: Each entry: { name: string, proficiency?: 'beginner'|'intermediate'|'advanced'|'expert' }. proficiency defaults to 'intermediate'.

data.chiStatus: 'generating' — CHI fires async. Poll GET /progress or GET /chi-explainer to check when ready.

errors.userMessage: Please enter your current or most recent job title.
Please enter the company name for your current or most recent role.
Please enter when you started this role (month and year).
Please select the role you're targeting, or type it in if you don't see it in the list.

errors.when: One or more expectedRoleIds not found in roles collection

POST /education-experience.description: P1-02 — Enrichment step. All fields are now optional (P1-02: no required field gates except basic structure). Accepts full education[], experience[], skills[], salary, seniority, career gaps. CHI recalculates after save with higher completeness score.
notes: P1-02: education[] and experience[] are no longer mutually required — at least one was previously enforced, now relaxed for enrichment-only calls
P1-03: currentSalaryLPA is fully optional. Use salaryDeclined:true to explicitly indicate non-disclosure.
The integer sentinel -1 is still accepted for backwards compatibility but salaryDeclined:true is preferred

education.description: Each entry: { qualificationId|qualificationName (one required), institution, yearOfGraduation?, grade?, specialization? }

experience.description: Each entry: { jobTitle, company (both required), startDate, endDate?, isCurrent?, responsibilities[]?, achievements[]?, industryId? }

expectedRoleIds.description: If provided, validated against roles collection. At least 1 if array is non-empty.

currentSalaryLPA.description: Annual salary in LPA. Optional. When absent, salaryTrajectory CHI dimension defaults to 40.

salaryDeclined.description: P1-03: Set true to explicitly indicate user declined to share salary. Preferred over -1 sentinel.

careerGaps.description: Each entry: { startDate (YYYY-MM), endDate (YYYY-MM), reason: 'education'|'personal'|'health'|'relocation'|'other', description? }

targetRoleId.description: Single primary target role. Validated against roles collection if provided.

errors.userMessage: Please select at least one role you're targeting.
Please enter a valid salary amount, or leave it blank to skip.

errors.when: experience entry missing jobTitle or company
education entry missing qualificationId/Name or institution

GET /suggest-roles.description: P1-05 — Returns role suggestions based on a free-text job title string. Used to pre-fill the expectedRoleIds[] picker in Quick Start. Results are cached 1 hour in memory.

q.description: Job title to match against. E.g. 'Product Manager', 'Software Engineer'.

suggestions.title: string
suggestions.confidence: number (0-100) — 100=exact match, 80=all tokens match, 60=prefix match, 40=partial match, 20=weak match

notes: Returns empty suggestions array (not 404) when q is blank or no matches found
Confidence score is for frontend display only — sort order already reflects relevance

GET /teaser-chi.description: P1-06 — Returns an industry-average CHI snapshot for a given job family. No user data needed. Shown on the onboarding entry screen before Step 1 as a value hook. Falls back to 'general' industry average if jobFamilyId not found.
GET /teaser-chi.auth: required (user must be logged in to reach onboarding, but no user data is read)

jobFamilyId.description: Job family ID. Available: software_engineering, product, data, sales, marketing, general (default).

data.topPercentile: number — score of top 25% in this family
data.callToAction: string — e.g. 'This is the industry average. Complete your profile to see where you stand.'

notes: analysisSource: 'teaser' — frontend should NEVER show this as the user's personal score
Never returns 404 — falls back to hardcoded fallback if teaserChi collection is not seeded

GET /progress.description: P1-08 — Returns the full onboarding progress state. Updated for two-phase model. Frontend uses this to route the user to the correct step, render progress bars, and show CHI enrichment nudges.

steps.label: string
steps.isCurrent: boolean — true for the step the user should act on next
steps.estimatedTimeMinutes: number — P1-08: time estimate for this step

data.nextRequiredStep: string | null — 'quick_start' | 'career_report' | null (when all required steps done)
data.quickStartCompleted: boolean — P1-08: true when POST /quick-start has been called
data.chiDataCompleteness: number (0-100) — how complete the CHI input data is
data.remainingQuota: object | null — free tier quota remaining
data.quotaExhausted: object | null — { careerReport: boolean, generateCV: boolean, upgradeUrl: string }
data.draft: object | null — saved draft from PATCH /draft
data.data: object — full raw onboardingProgress document (for debugging)

PATCH /draft.description: Auto-save endpoint. Accepts any partial subset of Quick Start or enrichment fields. No validation — stores as-is. Frontend calls this on blur/change to prevent data loss.

request.body: Any subset of Quick Start or education-experience fields. No required fields.

GET /roles/search.description: G-06 / P1-04 — Onboarding-optimised role search. Returns roles matching a text query with relevance scoring and job family grouping. Registered at GET /api/v1/roles/search (roles module, not onboarding module).

q.description: Text search across role title and aliases.

EducationEntry.qualificationId: string (one of qualificationId or qualificationName required)
EducationEntry.qualificationName: string (one of qualificationId or qualificationName required)

AchievementEntry.action: string (required) — what was done, e.g. 'Reduced latency'

migrationNotes.P1-02: POST /education-experience no longer requires both education[] and experience[]. All fields are now optional for enrichment calls.
migrationNotes.P1-03: currentSalaryLPA is now optional. Replace integer sentinel -1 with salaryDeclined:true. Both are still accepted.
migrationNotes.P1-10: targetRoleFreeText accepted on both POST /quick-start and POST /education-experience. Stored but not validated against Firestore roles.
migrationNotes.P0-04: All 4xx/422 error responses now include userMessage field when the error is user-facing. Frontend should prefer userMessage over message for display.
